channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Entri 1 : GoDaddy 0 - Court ruling allows Entri to challenge GoDaddy's alleged anti-competitive behaviour
item.description: The court’s decision to deny GoDaddy’s motion to dismiss the case indicates that Entri has presented a strong case worthy of further examination.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Optus once again crowned the fastest mobile network in latest report
item.description: Opensignal has released its latest report on Australian phone network experiences, and Optus has won the most awards once again.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work has begun on JDK 24, with six features so far proposed for the release: removal of the non-generational mode in the Z Garbage Collector, stream gatherers, a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What a Trump win could mean for Elon Musk's businesses
item.description: Elon Musk is wearing multiple hats this election season, sparking questions about how his dual role as a tech leader and vocal surrogate for former President Trump could lead to conflicts of interest.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI hires first chief economist
item.description: OpenAI hired its first-ever chief economist to examine the economic impacts of artificial intelligence (AI), the ChatGPT maker announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kamala Harris is going all in on Snapchat. But Donald Trump is still banned
item.description: Since July, Kamala Harris’s campaign has purchased more than 5,000 Snapchat ads, amounting to an estimated $​​5.3 million. It’s not hard to understand why: Snapchat is a haven for young people.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Wall Street Journal, New York Post sue AI startup
item.description: Media conglomerate News Corp, which owns The Wall Street Journal, New York Post and a number of other media properties, is suing artificial intelligence (AI) startup Perplexity over what it alleges is copyright infringement.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Why Python is the language of choice for AI
item.description: The widespread adoption of AI is creating a paradigm shift in the software engineering world. Python has quickly become the programming language of choice for AI development due to its usability, mature ecosystem, and ability to meet the data-driven needs of AI and machine learning (ML) workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Perplexity is sued by Dow Jones and ‘New York Post’ for copyright infringement
item.description: Rupert Murdoch’s Dow Jones, which is the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, and the New York Post both sued AI search startup Perplexity on Monday for infringing copyrighted content. The News Corp.-owned companies accused Perplexity of a “massive amount of illegal copying” of their work.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Michigan secretary of state pushes back against Musk's voter registration claims
item.description: Michigan Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson (D) and tech billionaire Elon Musk got into a days-long back-and-forth on the social platform X about voter registration in the Wolverine State.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rust 1.82: Enhancements and Improvements for Developers
item.description: Rust programming language, Cargo package manager registry, macOS on 64-bit Arm CPUs
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Republicans pushed Silicon Valley social media companies to stop fighting election misinformation
item.description: The shift took place against the backdrop of an intimidation campaign led by Republican state and federal lawmakers aimed at forcing social media companies to platform falsehoods and hate speech and thwarting those working to study or limit the spread of that destabilizing content.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5
